mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuation through lower discount rates and improved risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is still easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of months with a falling FEDFUNDS trend, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation slows sharply, real discount-rate pressure eases and the market assigns a lower probability to further tightening, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change falls below its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS is not rising
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the sign flips and returns are negative when inflation decelerates without a concurrent FEDFUNDS pause.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals slack labor demand and weaker earnings expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the following month as analysts cut profit forecasts.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day SPX returns are positive in more than 60% of UNRATE-rise trigger events, or if the signal has no negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS indicates stronger nominal final demand and better near-term revenue growth for large-cap firms, which should generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher RSAFS growth does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the sign becomes non-positive in consecutive expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Improving INDPRO with rising HOUST signals a synchronized supply-side recovery that reduces recession risk and supports cyclical equity multiples, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and HOUST fail to predict above-baseline 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the sign and the interaction adds no lift.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS contracts while UNRATE rises, the labor market is deteriorating fast enough to threaten consumption and margins, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls for 2 consecutive months and UNRATE rises at the same time
failure_condition: Reject if the combined deterioration trigger does not produce lower-than-baseline 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect disappears once PAYEMS and UNRATE are tested separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST below its recent trend marks a housing-demand slowdown that usually precedes broader cyclical weakening, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST breakdowns are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal vanishes outside housing-led downturns.

mechanism_narrative: In a restrictive policy regime, a high FEDFUNDS level combined with elevated CPIAUCSL keeps real financing costs tight and suppresses equity multiples, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 4.5% and CPIAUCSL inflation is still above its 12-month average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation trigger events do not produce negative 5-day SPX returns, or if returns are positive despite both series remaining elevated.
